name: EPA+DHA lowered inflammatory cytokines in aging adults with venous ulcers
summary: A randomized controlled study in middle-to-late adulthood adults with chronic venous leg ulcers found significantly lower IL-6, IL-1beta, and TNF-alpha after EPA+DHA versus control.
statement: In 35 adults with chronic venous leg ulcers and mean age about 60.6 years, EPA+DHA supplementation significantly lowered circulating IL-6, IL-1beta, and TNF-alpha compared with control at both week 4 and week 8.
population_or_model: Adults in middle to late adulthood with chronic venous leg ulcers
quantitative_note: Adjusted between-group p values were reported as IL-6 p=0.008, IL-1beta p<0.001, and TNF-alpha p<0.001 at week 4; IL-6 p=0.007, IL-1beta p<0.001, and TNF-alpha p<0.001 at week 8.
caveats: The endpoint is systemic inflammatory cytokine movement, not definitive wound healing.
EPA+DHA is a precursor strategy rather than a direct SPM therapeutic.
